verses.kjv: THE former treatise have I made, O Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,
verses.bsb: In my first book, O Theophilus, I wrote about all that Jesus began to do and to teach,

verses.kjv: Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:
verses.bsb: until the day He was taken up to heaven, after giving instructions through the Holy Spirit to the apostles He had chosen.

verses.kjv: To whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of God:
verses.bsb: After His suffering, He presented Himself to them with many convincing proofs that He was alive. He appeared to them over a span of forty days and spoke about the kingdom of God.

verses.kjv: And, being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, saith he, ye have heard of me.
verses.bsb: And while they were gathered together, He commanded them: “Do not leave Jerusalem, but wait for the gift the Father promised, which you have heard Me discuss.

verses.kjv: For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.
verses.bsb: For John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”

verses.kjv: When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to Israel?
verses.bsb: So when they came together, they asked Him, “Lord, will You at this time restore the kingdom to Israel?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “It is not for you to know times or seasons that the Father has fixed by His own authority.

verses.kjv: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.
verses.bsb: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you, and you will be My witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.”

verses.kjv: And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.
verses.bsb: After He had said this, they watched as He was taken up, and a cloud hid Him from their sight.

verses.kjv: And while they looked stedfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;
verses.bsb: They were looking intently into the sky as He was going, when suddenly two men dressed in white stood beside them.

verses.kjv: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
verses.bsb: “Men of Galilee,” they said, “why do you stand here looking into the sky? This same Jesus, who has been taken from you into heaven, will come back in the same way you have seen Him go into heaven.”

verses.kjv: Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day’s journey.
verses.bsb: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near the city, a Sabbath day’s journey away.

verses.kjv: And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James the son of Alphæus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas the brother of James.
verses.bsb: When they arrived, they went to the upper room where they were staying: Peter and John, James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas son of James.

verses.kjv: These all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.
verses.bsb: With one accord they all continued in prayer, along with the women and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with His brothers.

verses.kjv: ¶ And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)
verses.bsb: In those days Peter stood up among the brothers (a gathering of about a hundred and twenty) and said,

verses.kjv: Men and brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David spake before concerning Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.
verses.bsb: “Brothers, the Scripture had to be fulfilled which the Holy Spirit foretold through the mouth of David concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.

verses.kjv: For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.
verses.bsb: He was one of our number and shared in this ministry.”

verses.kjv: Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.
verses.bsb: (Now with the reward for his wickedness Judas bought a field; there he fell headlong and burst open in the middle, and all his intestines spilled out.

verses.kjv: And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.
verses.bsb: This became known to all who lived in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Akeldama, that is, Field of Blood.)

verses.kjv: For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.
verses.bsb: “For it is written in the book of Psalms:  ‘May his place be deserted; let there be no one to dwell in it,’   and,  ‘May another take his position.’

verses.kjv: Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
verses.bsb: Therefore it is necessary to choose one of the men who have accompanied us the whole time the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,

verses.kjv: Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection.
verses.bsb: beginning from John’s baptism until the day Jesus was taken up from us. For one of these must become a witness with us of His resurrection.”

verses.kjv: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
verses.bsb: So they proposed two men: Joseph called Barsabbas (also known as Justus) and Matthias.

verses.kjv: And they prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which knowest the hearts of all men, shew whether of these two thou hast chosen,
verses.bsb: And they prayed, “Lord, You know everyone’s heart. Show us which of these two You have chosen

verses.kjv: That he may take part of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas by transgression fell, that he might go to his own place.
verses.bsb: to take up this ministry and apostleship, which Judas abandoned to go to his rightful place.”

verses.kjv: And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
verses.bsb: Then they cast lots, and the lot fell to Matthias; so he was added to the eleven apostles.

verses.kjv: AND when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
verses.bsb: When the day of Pentecost came, they were all together in one place.

verses.kjv: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
verses.bsb: Suddenly a sound like a mighty rushing wind came from heaven and filled the whole house where they were sitting.

verses.kjv: And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.
verses.bsb: They saw tongues like flames of fire that separated and came to rest on each of them.

verses.kjv: And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.
verses.bsb: And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit enabled them.

verses.kjv: And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.
verses.bsb: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem God-fearing Jews from every nation under heaven.

verses.kjv: Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded, because that every man heard them speak in his own language.
verses.bsb: And when this sound rang out, a crowd came together in bewilderment, because each one heard them speaking his own language.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galilæans?
verses.bsb: Astounded and amazed, they asked, “Are not all these men who are speaking Galileans?

verses.kjv: And how hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?
verses.bsb: How is it then that each of us hears them in his own native language?

verses.kjv: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judæa, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia,
verses.bsb: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,

verses.kjv: Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene, and strangers of Rome, Jews and proselytes,
verses.bsb: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya near Cyrene; visitors from Rome,

verses.kjv: Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.
verses.bsb: both Jews and converts to Judaism; Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the wonders of God in our own tongues!”

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?
verses.bsb: Astounded and perplexed, they asked one another, “What does this mean?”

verses.kjv: Others mocking said, These men are full of new wine.
verses.bsb: But others mocked them and said, “They are drunk on new wine!”

verses.kjv: ¶ But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them, Ye men of Judæa, and all ye that dwell at Jerusalem, be this known unto you, and hearken to my words:
verses.bsb: Then Peter stood up with the Eleven, lifted up his voice, and addressed the crowd: “Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen carefully to my words.

verses.kjv: For these are not drunken, as ye suppose, seeing it is but the third hour of the day.
verses.bsb: These men are not drunk, as you suppose. It is only the third hour of the day!

verses.kjv: But this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel;
verses.bsb: No, this is what was spoken by the prophet Joel:

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams:
verses.bsb: ‘In the last days, God says, I will pour out My Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.

verses.kjv: And on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall prophesy:
verses.bsb: Even on My menservants and maidservants I will pour out My Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.

verses.kjv: And I will shew wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:
verses.bsb: I will show wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and billows of smoke.

verses.kjv: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and notable day of the Lord come:
verses.bsb: The sun will be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the coming of the great and glorious Day of the Lord.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.
verses.bsb: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.’

verses.kjv: Ye men of Israel, hear these words; Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved of God among you by miracles and wonders and signs, which God did by him in the midst of you, as ye yourselves also know:
verses.bsb: Men of Israel, listen to this message: Jesus of Nazareth was a man certified by God to you by miracles, wonders, and signs, which God did among you through Him, as you yourselves know.

verses.kjv: Him, being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have crucified and slain:
verses.bsb: He was delivered up by God’s set plan and foreknowledge, and you, by the hands of the lawless, put Him to death by nailing Him to the cross.

verses.kjv: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
verses.bsb: But God raised Him from the dead, releasing Him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for Him to be held in its clutches.

verses.kjv: For David speaketh concerning him, I foresaw the Lord always before my face, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved:
verses.bsb: David says about Him:  ‘I saw the Lord always before me; because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.

verses.kjv: Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
verses.bsb: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices; my body also will dwell in hope,

verses.kjv: Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
verses.bsb: because You will not abandon my soul to Hades, nor will You let Your Holy One see decay.

verses.kjv: Thou hast made known to me the ways of life; thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance.
verses.bsb: You have made known to me the paths of life; You will fill me with joy in Your presence.’

verses.kjv: Men and brethren, let me freely speak unto you of the patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried, and his sepulchre is with us unto this day.
verses.bsb: Brothers, I can tell you with confidence that the patriarch David died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.

verses.kjv: Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up Christ to sit on his throne;
verses.bsb: But he was a prophet and knew that God had promised him on oath that He would place one of his descendants on his throne.

verses.kjv: He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption.
verses.bsb: Foreseeing this, David spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that He was not abandoned to Hades, nor did His body see decay.

verses.kjv: This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we all are witnesses.
verses.bsb: God has raised this Jesus to life, to which we are all witnesses.

verses.kjv: Therefore being by the right hand of God exalted, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath shed forth this, which ye now see and hear.
verses.bsb: Exalted, then, to the right hand of God, He has received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit and has poured out what you now see and hear.

verses.kjv: For David is not ascended into the heavens: but he saith himself, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
verses.bsb: For David did not ascend into heaven, but he himself says:  ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand

verses.kjv: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
verses.bsb: until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.”’

verses.kjv: Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly, that God hath made that same Jesus, whom ye have crucified, both Lord and Christ.
verses.bsb: Therefore let all Israel know with certainty that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ!”

verses.kjv: ¶ Now when they heard this, they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, what shall we do?
verses.bsb: When the people heard this, they were cut to the heart and asked Peter and the other apostles, “Brothers, what shall we do?”

verses.kjv: Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Peter replied, “Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: For the promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the Lord our God shall call.
verses.bsb: This promise belongs to you and your children and to all who are far off—to all whom the Lord our God will call to Himself.”

verses.kjv: And with many other words did he testify and exhort, saying, Save yourselves from this untoward generation.
verses.bsb: With many other words he testified, and he urged them, “Be saved from this corrupt generation.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then they that gladly received his word were baptized: and the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
verses.bsb: Those who embraced his message were baptized, and about three thousand were added to the believers that day.

verses.kjv: And they continued stedfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers.
verses.bsb: They devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching and to the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.

verses.kjv: And fear came upon every soul: and many wonders and signs were done by the apostles.
verses.bsb: A sense of awe came over everyone, and the apostles performed many wonders and signs.

verses.kjv: And all that believed were together, and had all things common;
verses.bsb: All the believers were together and had everything in common.

verses.kjv: And sold their possessions and goods, and parted them to all men, as every man had need.
verses.bsb: Selling their possessions and goods, they shared with anyone who was in need.

verses.kjv: And they, continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart,
verses.bsb: With one accord they continued to meet daily in the temple courts and to break bread from house to house, sharing their meals with gladness and sincerity of heart,

verses.kjv: Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And the Lord added to the church daily such as should be saved.
verses.bsb: praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.

verses.kjv: NOW Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, being the ninth hour.
verses.bsb: One afternoon Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.

verses.kjv: And a certain man lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;
verses.bsb: And a man who was lame from birth was being carried to the temple gate called Beautiful, where he was put every day to beg from those entering the temple courts.

verses.kjv: Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an alms.
verses.bsb: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he asked them for money.

verses.kjv: And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.
verses.bsb: Peter looked directly at him, as did John. “Look at us!” said Peter.

verses.kjv: And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.
verses.bsb: So the man gave them his attention, expecting to receive something from them.

verses.kjv: Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have give I thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.
verses.bsb: But Peter said, “Silver or gold I do not have, but what I have I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!”

verses.kjv: And he took him by the right hand, and lifted him up: and immediately his feet and ancle bones received strength.
verses.bsb: Taking him by the right hand, Peter helped him up, and at once the man’s feet and ankles were made strong.

verses.kjv: And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
verses.bsb: He sprang to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and leaping and praising God.

verses.kjv: And all the people saw him walking and praising God:
verses.bsb: When all the people saw him walking and praising God,

verses.kjv: And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.
verses.bsb: they recognized him as the man who used to sit begging at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.

verses.kjv: And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.
verses.bsb: While the man clung to Peter and John, all the people were astonished and ran to them in the walkway called Solomon’s Colonnade.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Peter saw it, he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?
verses.bsb: And when Peter saw this, he addressed the people: “Men of Israel, why are you surprised by this? Why do you stare at us as if by our own power or godliness we had made this man walk?

verses.kjv: The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let him go.
verses.bsb: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified His servant Jesus. You handed Him over and rejected Him before Pilate, even though he had decided to release Him.

verses.kjv: But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a murderer to be granted unto you;
verses.bsb: You rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a murderer be released to you.

verses.kjv: And killed the Prince of life, whom God hath raised from the dead; whereof we are witnesses.
verses.bsb: You killed the Author of life, but God raised Him from the dead, and we are witnesses of the fact.

verses.kjv: And his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
verses.bsb: By faith in the name of Jesus, this man whom you see and know has been made strong. It is Jesus’ name and the faith that comes through Him that has given him this complete healing in your presence.

verses.kjv: And now, brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did it, as did also your rulers.
verses.bsb: And now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did your leaders.

verses.kjv: But those things, which God before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he hath so fulfilled.
verses.bsb: But in this way God has fulfilled what He foretold through all the prophets, saying that His Christ would suffer.

verses.kjv: ¶ Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord;
verses.bsb: Repent, then, and turn back, so that your sins may be wiped away,

verses.kjv: And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:
verses.bsb: that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that He may send Jesus, the Christ, who has been appointed for you.

verses.kjv: Whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.
verses.bsb: Heaven must take Him in until the time comes for the restoration of all things, which God announced long ago through His holy prophets.

verses.kjv: For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.
verses.bsb: For Moses said, ‘The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers. You must listen to Him in everything He tells you.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be destroyed from among the people.
verses.bsb: Everyone who does not listen to Him will be completely cut off from among his people.’

verses.kjv: Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise foretold of these days.
verses.bsb: Indeed, all the prophets from Samuel on, as many as have spoken, have proclaimed these days.

verses.kjv: Ye are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.
verses.bsb: And you are sons of the prophets and of the covenant God made with your fathers when He said to Abraham, ‘Through your offspring all the families of the earth will be blessed.’

verses.kjv: Unto you first God, having raised up his Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.
verses.bsb: When God raised up His Servant, He sent Him first to you to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways.”

verses.kjv: AND as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,
verses.bsb: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests and the captain of the temple guard and the Sadducees came up to them,

verses.kjv: Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection from the dead.
verses.bsb: greatly disturbed that they were teaching the people and proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection of the dead.

verses.kjv: And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.
verses.bsb: They seized Peter and John, and because it was evening, they put them in custody until the next day.

verses.kjv: Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.
verses.bsb: But many who heard the message believed, and the number of men grew to about five thousand.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes,
verses.bsb: The next day the rulers, elders, and scribes assembled in Jerusalem,

verses.kjv: And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and many others from the high priest’s family.

verses.kjv: And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?
verses.bsb: They had Peter and John brought in and began to question them: “By what power or what name did you do this?”

verses.kjv: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
verses.bsb: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, “Rulers and elders of the people!

verses.kjv: If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;
verses.bsb: If we are being examined today about a kind service to a man who was lame, to determine how he was healed,

verses.kjv: Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, even by him doth this man stand here before you whole.
verses.bsb: then let this be known to all of you and to all the people of Israel: It is by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead, that this man stands before you healed.

verses.kjv: This is the stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.
verses.bsb: This Jesus is  ‘the stone you builders rejected, which has become the cornerstone.’

verses.kjv: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
verses.bsb: Salvation exists in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to men by which we must be saved.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.
verses.bsb: When they saw the boldness of Peter and John and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they marveled and took note that these men had been with Jesus.

verses.kjv: And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.
verses.bsb: And seeing the man who had been healed standing there with them, they had nothing to say in response.

verses.kjv: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,
verses.bsb: So they ordered them to leave the Sanhedrin and then conferred together.

verses.kjv: Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny it.
verses.bsb: “What shall we do with these men?” they asked. “It is clear to everyone living in Jerusalem that a remarkable miracle has occurred through them, and we cannot deny it.

verses.kjv: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.
verses.bsb: But to keep this message from spreading any further among the people, we must warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.”

verses.kjv: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then they called them in again and commanded them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.

verses.kjv: But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.
verses.bsb: But Peter and John replied, “Judge for yourselves whether it is right in God’s sight to listen to you rather than God.

verses.kjv: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.
verses.bsb: For we cannot stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.”

verses.kjv: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.
verses.bsb: After further threats they let them go. They could not find a way to punish them, because all the people were glorifying God for what had happened.

verses.kjv: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.
verses.bsb: For the man who was miraculously healed was over forty years old.

verses.kjv: ¶ And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.
verses.bsb: On their release, Peter and John returned to their own people and reported everything that the chief priests and elders had said to them.

verses.kjv: And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou art God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:
verses.bsb: When the believers heard this, they lifted up their voices to God with one accord. “Sovereign Lord,” they said, “You made the heaven and the earth and the sea and everything in them.

verses.kjv: Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
verses.bsb: You spoke by the Holy Spirit through the mouth of Your servant, our father David:  ‘Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain?

verses.kjv: The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.
verses.bsb: The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together against the Lord and against His Anointed One.’

verses.kjv: For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,
verses.bsb: In fact, this is the very city where Herod and Pontius Pilate conspired with the Gentiles and the people of Israel against Your holy servant Jesus, whom You anointed.

verses.kjv: For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.
verses.bsb: They carried out what Your hand and will had decided beforehand would happen.

verses.kjv: And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
verses.bsb: And now, Lord, consider their threats, and enable Your servants to speak Your word with complete boldness,

verses.kjv: By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.
verses.bsb: as You stretch out Your hand to heal and perform signs and wonders through the name of Your holy servant Jesus.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.
verses.bsb: After they had prayed, their meeting place was shaken, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God boldly.

verses.kjv: And the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul: neither said any of them that ought of the things which he possessed was his own; but they had all things common.
verses.bsb: The multitude of believers was one in heart and soul. No one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but they shared everything they owned.

verses.kjv: And with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great grace was upon them all.
verses.bsb: With great power the apostles continued to give their testimony about the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. And abundant grace was upon them all.

verses.kjv: Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,
verses.bsb: There were no needy ones among them, because those who owned lands or houses would sell their property, bring the proceeds from the sales,

verses.kjv: And laid them down at the apostles’ feet: and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need.
verses.bsb: and lay them at the apostles’ feet for distribution to anyone as he had need.

verses.kjv: And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus,
verses.bsb: Joseph, a Levite from Cyprus, whom the apostles called Barnabas (meaning Son of Encouragement),

verses.kjv: Having land, sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
verses.bsb: sold a field he owned, brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

verses.kjv: BUT a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
verses.bsb: Now a man named Ananias, together with his wife Sapphira, also sold a piece of property.

verses.kjv: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
verses.bsb: With his wife’s full knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds for himself, but brought a portion and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

verses.kjv: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?
verses.bsb: Then Peter said, “Ananias, how is it that Satan has filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and withhold some of the proceeds from the land?

verses.kjv: Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.
verses.bsb: Did it not belong to you before it was sold? And after it was sold, was it not at your disposal? How could you conceive such a deed in your heart? You have not lied to men, but to God!”

verses.kjv: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
verses.bsb: On hearing these words, Ananias fell down and died. And great fear came over all who heard what had happened.

verses.kjv: And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.
verses.bsb: Then the young men stepped forward, wrapped up his body, and carried him out and buried him.

verses.kjv: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
verses.bsb: About three hours later his wife also came in, unaware of what had happened.

verses.kjv: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.
verses.bsb: “Tell me,” said Peter, “is this the price you and your husband got for the land?”  “Yes,” she answered, “that is the price.”

verses.kjv: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.
verses.bsb: “How could you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord?” Peter replied. “Look, the feet of the men who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out also.”

verses.kjv: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
verses.bsb: At that instant she fell down at his feet and died. Then the young men came in and, finding her dead, carried her out and buried her beside her husband.

verses.kjv: And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
verses.bsb: And great fear came over the whole church and all who heard about these events.

verses.kjv: ¶ And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
verses.bsb: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people, and with one accord the believers gathered together in Solomon’s Colonnade.

verses.kjv: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
verses.bsb: Although the people regarded them highly, no one else dared to join them.

verses.kjv: And believers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
verses.bsb: Yet more and more believers were brought to the Lord—large numbers of both men and women.

verses.kjv: Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
verses.bsb: As a result, people brought the sick into the streets and laid them on cots and mats, so that at least Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by.

verses.kjv: There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed every one.
verses.bsb: Crowds also gathered from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean spirits, and all of them were healed.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with indignation,
verses.bsb: Then the high priest and all his associates, who belonged to the party of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy. They went out

verses.kjv: And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
verses.bsb: and arrested the apostles and put them in the public jail.

verses.kjv: But the angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
verses.bsb: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the jail and brought them out, saying,

verses.kjv: Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
verses.bsb: “Go, stand in the temple courts and tell the people the full message of this new life.”

verses.kjv: And when they heard that, they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
verses.bsb: At daybreak the apostles entered the temple courts as they had been told and began to teach the people.  When the high priest and his associates arrived, they convened the Sanhedrin—the full assembly of the elders of Israel—and sent to the jail for the apostles.

verses.kjv: But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
verses.bsb: But on arriving at the jail, the officers did not find them there. So they returned with the report:

verses.kjv: Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
verses.bsb: “We found the jail securely locked, with the guards posted at the doors; but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”

verses.kjv: Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
verses.bsb: When the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests heard this account, they were perplexed as to what was happening.

verses.kjv: Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
verses.bsb: Then someone came in and announced, “Look, the men you put in jail are standing in the temple courts teaching the people!”

verses.kjv: Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
verses.bsb: At that point, the captain went with the officers and brought the apostles—but not by force, for fear the people would stone them.

verses.kjv: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council: and the high priest asked them,
verses.bsb: They brought them in and made them stand before the Sanhedrin, where the high priest interrogated them.

verses.kjv: Saying, Did not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s blood upon us.
verses.bsb: “We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name,” he said. “Yet you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and are determined to make us responsible for this man’s blood.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter and the other apostles answered and said, We ought to obey God rather than men.
verses.bsb: But Peter and the other apostles replied, “We must obey God rather than men.

verses.kjv: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and hanged on a tree.
verses.bsb: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom you had killed by hanging Him on a tree.

verses.kjv: Him hath God exalted with his right hand to be a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
verses.bsb: God exalted Him to His right hand as Prince and Savior, in order to grant repentance and forgiveness of sins to Israel.

verses.kjv: And we are his witnesses of these things; and so is also the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to them that obey him.
verses.bsb: We are witnesses of these things, and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey Him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ When they heard that, they were cut to the heart, and took counsel to slay them.
verses.bsb: When the Council members heard this, they were enraged, and they resolved to put the apostles to death.

verses.kjv: Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
verses.bsb: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law who was honored by all the people, stood up in the Sanhedrin and ordered that the men be put outside for a short time.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.
verses.bsb: “Men of Israel,” he said, “consider carefully what you are about to do to these men.

verses.kjv: For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
verses.bsb: Some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and about four hundred men joined him. He was killed, all his followers were dispersed, and it all came to nothing.

verses.kjv: After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, even as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
verses.bsb: After him, Judas the Galilean appeared in the days of the census and drew away people after him. He too perished, and all his followers were scattered.

verses.kjv: And now I say unto you, Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of men, it will come to nought:
verses.bsb: So in the present case I advise you: Leave these men alone. Let them go! For if their purpose or endeavor is of human origin, it will fail.

verses.kjv: But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; lest haply ye be found even to fight against God.
verses.bsb: But if it is from God, you will not be able to stop them. You may even find yourselves fighting against God.”

verses.kjv: And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
verses.bsb: At this, they yielded to Gamaliel. They called the apostles in and had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and released them.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name.
verses.bsb: The apostles left the Sanhedrin, rejoicing that they had been counted worthy of suffering disgrace for the Name.

verses.kjv: And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: Every day, in the temple courts and from house to house, they did not stop teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Christ.

verses.kjv: AND in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplied, there arose a murmuring of the Grecians against the Hebrews, because their widows were neglected in the daily ministration.
verses.bsb: In those days when the disciples were increasing in number, the Grecian Jews among them began to grumble against the Hebraic Jews because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food.

verses.kjv: Then the twelve called the multitude of the disciples unto them, and said, It is not reason that we should leave the word of God, and serve tables.
verses.bsb: So the Twelve summoned all the disciples and said, “It is unacceptable for us to neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables.

verses.kjv: Wherefore, brethren, look ye out among you seven men of honest report, full of the Holy Ghost and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.
verses.bsb: Therefore, brothers, select from among you seven men confirmed to be full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will appoint this responsibility to them

verses.kjv: But we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the ministry of the word.
verses.bsb: and will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a proselyte of Antioch:
verses.bsb: This proposal pleased the whole group. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, as well as Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.

verses.kjv: Whom they set before the apostles: and when they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.
verses.bsb: They presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.

verses.kjv: And the word of God increased; and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem greatly; and a great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.
verses.bsb: So the word of God continued to spread. The number of disciples in Jerusalem grew rapidly, and a great number of priests became obedient to the faith.

verses.kjv: And Stephen, full of faith and power, did great wonders and miracles among the people.
verses.bsb: Now Stephen, who was full of grace and power, was performing great wonders and signs among the people.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then there arose certain of the synagogue, which is called the synagogue of the Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia and of Asia, disputing with Stephen.
verses.bsb: But resistance arose from what was called the Synagogue of the Freedmen, including Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and men from the provinces of Cilicia and Asia. They began to argue with Stephen,

verses.kjv: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit by which he spake.
verses.bsb: but they could not stand up to his wisdom or the Spirit by whom he spoke.

verses.kjv: Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
verses.bsb: Then they prompted some men to say, “We heard Stephen speak words of blasphemy against Moses and against God.”

verses.kjv: And they stirred up the people, and the elders, and the scribes, and came upon him, and caught him, and brought him to the council,
verses.bsb: So they stirred up the people, elders, and scribes and confronted Stephen. They seized him and brought him before the Sanhedrin,

verses.kjv: And set up false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place, and the law:
verses.bsb: where they presented false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking against this holy place and against the law.

verses.kjv: For we have heard him say, that this Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the customs which Moses delivered us.
verses.bsb: For we have heard him say that Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.”

verses.kjv: And all that sat in the council, looking stedfastly on him, saw his face as it had been the face of an angel.
verses.bsb: All who were sitting in the Sanhedrin looked intently at Stephen, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

verses.kjv: THEN said the high priest, Are these things so?
verses.bsb: Then the high priest asked Stephen, “Are these charges true?”

verses.kjv: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
verses.bsb: And Stephen declared: “Brothers and fathers, listen to me! The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,

verses.kjv: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
verses.bsb: and told him, ‘Leave your country and your kindred and go to the land I will show you.’

verses.kjv: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldæans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.
verses.bsb: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, God brought him out of that place and into this land where you are now living.

verses.kjv: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.
verses.bsb: He gave him no inheritance here, not even a foot of ground. But God promised to give possession of the land to Abraham and his descendants, even though he did not yet have a child.

verses.kjv: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
verses.bsb: God told him that his descendants would be foreigners in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.

verses.kjv: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
verses.bsb: ‘But I will punish the nation that enslaves them,’ God said, ‘and afterward they will come forth and worship Me in this place.’

verses.kjv: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve patriarchs.
verses.bsb: Then God gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision, and Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day. And Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.

verses.kjv: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
verses.bsb: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him as a slave into Egypt. But God was with him

verses.kjv: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
verses.bsb: and rescued him from all his troubles. He granted Joseph favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and all his household.

verses.kjv: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
verses.bsb: Then famine and great suffering swept across Egypt and Canaan, and our fathers could not find food.

verses.kjv: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
verses.bsb: When Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our fathers on their first visit.

verses.kjv: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: On their second visit, Joseph revealed his identity to his brothers, and his family became known to Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives, seventy-five in all.

verses.kjv: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
verses.bsb: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our fathers died.

verses.kjv: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
verses.bsb: Their bones were carried back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor at Shechem for a price he paid in silver.

verses.kjv: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
verses.bsb: As the time drew near for God to fulfill His promise to Abraham, our people in Egypt increased greatly in number.

verses.kjv: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
verses.bsb: Then another king, who knew nothing of Joseph, arose over Egypt.

verses.kjv: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
verses.bsb: He exploited our people and oppressed our fathers, forcing them to abandon their infants so they would die.

verses.kjv: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house three months:
verses.bsb: At that time Moses was born, and he was beautiful in the sight of God. For three months he was nurtured in his father’s house.

verses.kjv: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
verses.bsb: When he was set outside, Pharaoh’s daughter took him and brought him up as her own son.

verses.kjv: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
verses.bsb: So Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in speech and action.

verses.kjv: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: When Moses was forty years old, he decided to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.

verses.kjv: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
verses.bsb: And when he saw one of them being mistreated, Moses went to his defense and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian who was oppressing him.

verses.kjv: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
verses.bsb: He assumed his brothers would understand that God was using him to deliver them, but they did not.

verses.kjv: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
verses.bsb: The next day he came upon two Israelites who were fighting, and he tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you mistreating each other?’

verses.kjv: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
verses.bsb: But the man who was abusing his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, ‘Who made you ruler and judge over us?

verses.kjv: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
verses.bsb: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

verses.kjv: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
verses.bsb: At this remark, Moses fled to the land of Midian, where he lived as a foreigner and had two sons.

verses.kjv: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
verses.bsb: After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to Moses in the flames of a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.

verses.kjv: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the Lord came unto him,
verses.bsb: When Moses saw it, he marveled at the sight. As he approached to look more closely, the voice of the Lord came to him:

verses.kjv: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
verses.bsb: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses trembled with fear and did not dare to look.

verses.kjv: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
verses.bsb: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.

verses.kjv: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
verses.bsb: I have indeed seen the oppression of My people in Egypt. I have heard their groaning and have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you back to Egypt.’

verses.kjv: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
verses.bsb: This Moses, whom they had rejected with the words, ‘Who made you ruler and judge?’ is the one whom God sent to be their ruler and redeemer through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

verses.kjv: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
verses.bsb: He led them out and performed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and for forty years in the wilderness.

verses.kjv: ¶ This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
verses.bsb: This is the same Moses who told the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers.’

verses.kjv: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
verses.bsb: He was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers. And he received living words to pass on to us.

verses.kjv: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
verses.bsb: But our fathers refused to obey him. Instead, they rejected him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.

verses.kjv: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
verses.bsb: They said to Aaron, ‘Make us gods who will go before us! As for this Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’

verses.kjv: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
verses.bsb: At that time they made a calf and offered a sacrifice to the idol, rejoicing in the works of their hands.

verses.kjv: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: But God turned away from them and gave them over to the worship of the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets:  ‘Did you bring Me sacrifices and offerings forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

verses.kjv: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.
verses.bsb: You have taken along the tabernacle of Molech and the star of your god Rephan, the idols you made to worship. Therefore I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.’

verses.kjv: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.
verses.bsb: Our fathers had the tabernacle of the Testimony with them in the wilderness. It was constructed exactly as God had directed Moses, according to the pattern he had seen.

verses.kjv: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
verses.bsb: And our fathers who received it brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations God drove out before them. It remained until the time of David,

verses.kjv: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: who found favor in the sight of God and asked to provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.

verses.kjv: But Solomon built him an house.
verses.bsb: But it was Solomon who built the house for Him.

verses.kjv: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
verses.bsb: However, the Most High does not dwell in houses made by human hands. As the prophet says:

verses.kjv: Heaven is my throne, and earth is my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what is the place of my rest?
verses.bsb: ‘Heaven is My throne and the earth is My footstool. What kind of house will you build for Me, says the Lord, or where will My place of repose be?

verses.kjv: Hath not my hand made all these things?
verses.bsb: Has not My hand made all these things?’

verses.kjv: ¶ Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers did, so do ye.
verses.bsb: You stiff-necked people with uncircumcised hearts and ears! You always resist the Holy Spirit, just as your fathers did.

verses.kjv: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:
verses.bsb: Which of the prophets did your fathers fail to persecute? They even killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One. And now you are His betrayers and murderers—

verses.kjv: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept it.
verses.bsb: you who received the law ordained by angels, yet have not kept it.”

verses.kjv: ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, the members of the Sanhedrin were enraged, and they gnashed their teeth at him.

verses.kjv: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,
verses.bsb: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, looked intently into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.

verses.kjv: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.
verses.bsb: “Look,” he said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”

verses.kjv: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,
verses.bsb: At this they covered their ears, cried out in a loud voice, and rushed together at him.

verses.kjv: And cast him out of the city, and stoned him: and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man’s feet, whose name was Saul.
verses.bsb: They dragged him out of the city and began to stone him. Meanwhile the witnesses laid their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

verses.kjv: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon God, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
verses.bsb: While they were stoning him, Stephen appealed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”

verses.kjv: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
verses.bsb: Falling on his knees, he cried out in a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

verses.kjv: AND Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judæa and Samaria, except the apostles.
verses.bsb: And Saul was there, giving approval to Stephen’s death.  On that day a great persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout Judea and Samaria.

verses.kjv: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great lamentation over him.
verses.bsb: God-fearing men buried Stephen and mourned deeply over him.

verses.kjv: As for Saul, he made havock of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed them to prison.
verses.bsb: But Saul began to destroy the church. Going from house to house, he dragged off men and women and put them in prison.

verses.kjv: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.
verses.bsb: Those who had been scattered preached the word wherever they went.

verses.kjv: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.
verses.bsb: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and proclaimed the Christ to them.

verses.kjv: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
verses.bsb: The crowds gave their undivided attention to Philip’s message and to the signs they saw him perform.

verses.kjv: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.
verses.bsb: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many who were possessed, and many of the paralyzed and lame were healed.

verses.kjv: And there was great joy in that city.
verses.bsb: So there was great joy in that city.

verses.kjv: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:
verses.bsb: Prior to that time, a man named Simon had practiced sorcery in the city and astounded the people of Samaria. He claimed to be someone great,

verses.kjv: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
verses.bsb: and all the people, from the least to the greatest, heeded his words and said, “This man is the divine power called the Great Power.”

verses.kjv: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.
verses.bsb: They paid close attention to him because he had astounded them for a long time with his sorcery.

verses.kjv: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
verses.bsb: But when they believed Philip as he preached the gospel of the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

verses.kjv: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.
verses.bsb: Even Simon himself believed and was baptized. He followed Philip closely and was astounded by the great signs and miracles he observed.

verses.kjv: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:
verses.bsb: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.

verses.kjv: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:
verses.bsb: On their arrival, they prayed for them to receive the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
verses.bsb: For the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had simply been baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.

verses.kjv: Then laid they their hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles’ hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
verses.bsb: When Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money.

verses.kjv: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: “Give me this power as well,” he said, “so that everyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”

verses.kjv: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.
verses.bsb: But Peter replied, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could buy the gift of God with money!

verses.kjv: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
verses.bsb: You have no part or share in our ministry, because your heart is not right before God.

verses.kjv: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.
verses.bsb: Repent, therefore, of your wickedness, and pray to the Lord. Perhaps He will forgive you for the intent of your heart.

verses.kjv: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.
verses.bsb: For I see that you are poisoned by bitterness and captive to iniquity.”

verses.kjv: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.
verses.bsb: Then Simon answered, “Pray to the Lord for me, so that nothing you have said may happen to me.”

verses.kjv: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
verses.bsb: And after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many of the Samaritan villages.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.
verses.bsb: Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go south to the desert road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.”

verses.kjv: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,
verses.bsb: So he started out, and on his way he met an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official in charge of the entire treasury of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians. He had gone to Jerusalem to worship,

verses.kjv: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.
verses.bsb: and on his return was sitting in his chariot reading Isaiah the prophet.

verses.kjv: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.
verses.bsb: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go over to that chariot and stay by it.”

verses.kjv: And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
verses.bsb: So Philip ran up and heard the man reading Isaiah the prophet. “Do you understand what you are reading?” Philip asked.

verses.kjv: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.
verses.bsb: “How can I,” he said, “unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.

verses.kjv: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
verses.bsb: The eunuch was reading this passage of Scripture:  “He was led like a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before the shearer is silent, so He did not open His mouth.

verses.kjv: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.
verses.bsb: In His humiliation He was deprived of justice. Who can recount His descendants? For His life was removed from the earth.”

verses.kjv: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?
verses.bsb: “Tell me,” said the eunuch, “who is the prophet talking about, himself or someone else?”

verses.kjv: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then Philip began with this very Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus.

verses.kjv: And as they went on their way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, here is water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?
verses.bsb: As they traveled along the road and came to some water, the eunuch said, “Look, here is water! What is there to prevent me from being baptized?”

verses.kjv: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.
verses.bsb: And he gave orders to stop the chariot. Then both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.

verses.kjv: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.
verses.bsb: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, but went on his way rejoicing.

verses.kjv: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Cæsarea.
verses.bsb: But Philip appeared at Azotus and traveled through that region, preaching the gospel in all the towns until he came to Caesarea.

verses.kjv: AND Saul, yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went unto the high priest,
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Saul was still breathing out murderous threats against the disciples of the Lord. He approached the high priest

verses.kjv: And desired of him letters to Damascus to the synagogues, that if he found any of this way, whether they were men or women, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: and requested letters to the synagogues in Damascus, so that if he found any men or women belonging to the Way, he could bring them as prisoners to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And as he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven:
verses.bsb: As Saul drew near to Damascus on his journey, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.

verses.kjv: And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
verses.bsb: He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me?”

verses.kjv: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.
verses.bsb: “Who are You, Lord?” Saul asked.  “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting,” He replied.

verses.kjv: And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.
verses.bsb: “Now get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.”

verses.kjv: And the men which journeyed with him stood speechless, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.
