verses.bsb: But Paul said to the officers, “They beat us publicly without a trial and threw us into prison, even though we are Roman citizens. And now do they want to send us away secretly? Absolutely not! Let them come themselves and escort us out!”

verses.kjv: And the serjeants told these words unto the magistrates: and they feared, when they heard that they were Romans.
verses.bsb: So the officers relayed this message to the magistrates, who were alarmed to hear that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.

verses.kjv: And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the city.
verses.bsb: They came to appease them and led them out, requesting that they leave the city.

verses.kjv: And they went out of the prison, and entered into the house of Lydia: and when they had seen the brethren, they comforted them, and departed.
verses.bsb: After Paul and Silas came out of the prison, they went to Lydia’s house to see the brothers and encourage them. Then they left the city.

verses.kjv: NOW when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Jews:
verses.bsb: When they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

verses.kjv: And Paul, as his manner was, went in unto them, and three sabbath days reasoned with them out of the scriptures,
verses.bsb: As was his custom, Paul went into the synagogue, and on three Sabbaths he reasoned with them from the Scriptures,

verses.kjv: Opening and alleging, that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen again from the dead; and that this Jesus, whom I preach unto you, is Christ.
verses.bsb: explaining and proving that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. “This Jesus I am proclaiming to you is the Christ,” he declared.

verses.kjv: And some of them believed, and consorted with Paul and Silas; and of the devout Greeks a great multitude, and of the chief women not a few.
verses.bsb: Some of the Jews were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, along with a large number of God-fearing Greeks and quite a few leading women.

verses.kjv: ¶ But the Jews which believed not, moved with envy, took unto them certain lewd fellows of the baser sort, and gathered a company, and set all the city on an uproar, and assaulted the house of Jason, and sought to bring them out to the people.
verses.bsb: The Jews, however, became jealous. So they brought in some troublemakers from the marketplace, formed a mob, and sent the city into an uproar. They raided Jason’s house in search of Paul and Silas, hoping to bring them out to the people.

verses.kjv: And when they found them not, they drew Jason and certain brethren unto the rulers of the city, crying, These that have turned the world upside down are come hither also;
verses.bsb: But when they could not find them, they dragged Jason and some other brothers before the city officials, shouting, “These men who have turned the world upside down have now come here,

verses.kjv: Whom Jason hath received: and these all do contrary to the decrees of Cæsar, saying that there is another king, one Jesus.
verses.bsb: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. They are all defying Caesar’s decrees, saying that there is another king, named Jesus!”

verses.kjv: And they troubled the people and the rulers of the city, when they heard these things.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, the crowd and city officials were greatly disturbed.

verses.kjv: And when they had taken security of Jason, and of the other, they let them go.
verses.bsb: And they collected bond from Jason and the others, and then released them.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the brethren immediately sent away Paul and Silas by night unto Berea: who coming thither went into the synagogue of the Jews.
verses.bsb: As soon as night had fallen, the brothers sent Paul and Silas away to Berea. On arriving there, they went into the Jewish synagogue.

verses.kjv: These were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they received the word with all readiness of mind, and searched the scriptures daily, whether those things were so.
verses.bsb: Now the Bereans were more noble-minded than the Thessalonians, for they received the message with great eagerness and examined the Scriptures every day to see if these teachings were true.

verses.kjv: Therefore many of them believed; also of honourable women which were Greeks, and of men, not a few.
verses.bsb: As a result, many of them believed, along with quite a few prominent Greek women and men.

verses.kjv: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was preached of Paul at Berea, they came thither also, and stirred up the people.
verses.bsb: But when the Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul was also proclaiming the word of God in Berea, they went there themselves to incite and agitate the crowds.

verses.kjv: And then immediately the brethren sent away Paul to go as it were to the sea: but Silas and Timotheus abode there still.
verses.bsb: The brothers immediately sent Paul to the coast, but Silas and Timothy remained in Berea.

verses.kjv: And they that conducted Paul brought him unto Athens: and receiving a commandment unto Silas and Timotheus for to come to him with all speed, they departed.
verses.bsb: Those who escorted Paul brought him to Athens and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was stirred in him, when he saw the city wholly given to idolatry.
verses.bsb: While Paul was waiting for them in Athens, he was deeply disturbed in his spirit to see that the city was full of idols.

verses.kjv: Therefore disputed he in the synagogue with the Jews, and with the devout persons, and in the market daily with them that met with him.
verses.bsb: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and God-fearing Gentiles, and in the marketplace with those he met each day.

verses.kjv: Then certain philosophers of the Epicureans, and of the Stoicks, encountered him. And some said, What will this babbler say? other some, He seemeth to be a setter forth of strange gods: because he preached unto them Jesus, and the resurrection.
verses.bsb: Some Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also began to debate with him. Some of them asked, “What is this babbler trying to say?” Others said, “He seems to be advocating foreign gods.” They said this because Paul was proclaiming the good news of Jesus and the resurrection.

verses.kjv: And they took him, and brought him unto Areopagus, saying, May we know what this new doctrine, whereof thou speakest, is?
verses.bsb: So they took Paul and brought him to the Areopagus, where they asked him, “May we know what this new teaching is that you are presenting?

verses.kjv: For thou bringest certain strange things to our ears: we would know therefore what these things mean.
verses.bsb: For you are bringing some strange notions to our ears, and we want to know what they mean.”

verses.kjv: (For all the Athenians and strangers which were there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell, or to hear some new thing.)
verses.bsb: Now all the Athenians and foreigners who lived there spent their time doing nothing more than hearing and articulating new ideas.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Paul stood in the midst of Mars’ hill, and said, Ye men of Athens, I perceive that in all things ye are too superstitious.
verses.bsb: Then Paul stood up in the meeting of the Areopagus and said, “Men of Athens, I see that in every way you are very religious.

verses.kjv: For as I passed by, and beheld your devotions, I found an altar with this inscription, TO THE UNKNOWN GOD. Whom therefore ye ignorantly worship, him declare I unto you.
verses.bsb: For as I walked around and examined your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription:  TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.  Therefore what you worship as something unknown, I now proclaim to you.

verses.kjv: God that made the world and all things therein, seeing that he is Lord of heaven and earth, dwelleth not in temples made with hands;
verses.bsb: The God who made the world and everything in it is the Lord of heaven and earth and does not live in temples made by human hands.

verses.kjv: Neither is worshipped with men’s hands, as though he needed any thing, seeing he giveth to all life, and breath, and all things;
verses.bsb: Nor is He served by human hands, as if He needed anything, because He Himself gives everyone life and breath and everything else.

verses.kjv: And hath made of one blood all nations of men for to dwell on all the face of the earth, and hath determined the times before appointed, and the bounds of their habitation;
verses.bsb: From one man He made every nation of men, that they should inhabit the whole earth; and He determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.

verses.kjv: That they should seek the Lord, if haply they might feel after him, and find him, though he be not far from every one of us:
verses.bsb: God intended that they would seek Him and perhaps reach out for Him and find Him, though He is not far from each one of us.

verses.kjv: For in him we live, and move, and have our being; as certain also of your own poets have said, For we are also his offspring.
verses.bsb: ‘For in Him we live and move and have our being.’ As some of your own poets have said, ‘We are His offspring.’

verses.kjv: Forasmuch then as we are the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Godhead is like unto gold, or silver, or stone, graven by art and man’s device.
verses.bsb: Therefore, being offspring of God, we should not think that the Divine Being is like gold or silver or stone, an image formed by man’s skill and imagination.

verses.kjv: And the times of this ignorance God winked at; but now commandeth all men every where to repent:
verses.bsb: Although God overlooked the ignorance of earlier times, He now commands all people everywhere to repent.

verses.kjv: Because he hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained; whereof he hath given assurance unto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.
verses.bsb: For He has set a day when He will judge the world with justice by the Man He has appointed. He has given proof of this to everyone by raising Him from the dead.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked: and others said, We will hear thee again of this matter.
verses.bsb: When they heard about the resurrection of the dead, some began to mock him, but others said, “We want to hear you again on this topic.”

verses.kjv: So Paul departed from among them.
verses.bsb: At that, Paul left the Areopagus.

verses.kjv: Howbeit certain men clave unto him, and believed: among the which was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
verses.bsb: But some joined him and believed, including Dionysius the Areopagite, a woman named Damaris, and others who were with them.

verses.kjv: AFTER these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth;
verses.bsb: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.

verses.kjv: And found a certain Jew named Aquila, born in Pontus, lately come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla; (because that Claudius had commanded all Jews to depart from Rome:) and came unto them.
verses.bsb: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to leave Rome. Paul went to visit them,

verses.kjv: And because he was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought: for by their occupation they were tentmakers.
verses.bsb: and he stayed and worked with them because they were tentmakers by trade, just as he was.

verses.kjv: And he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath, and persuaded the Jews and the Greeks.
verses.bsb: Every Sabbath he reasoned in the synagogue, trying to persuade Jews and Greeks alike.

verses.kjv: And when Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in the spirit, and testified to the Jews that Jesus was Christ.
verses.bsb: And when Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus is the Christ.

verses.kjv: And when they opposed themselves, and blasphemed, he shook his raiment, and said unto them, Your blood be upon your own heads; I am clean: from henceforth I will go unto the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: But when they opposed and insulted him, he shook out his garments and told them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am innocent of it. From now on I will go to the Gentiles.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he departed thence, and entered into a certain man’s house, named Justus, one that worshipped God, whose house joined hard to the synagogue.
verses.bsb: So Paul left the synagogue and went next door to the house of Titus Justus, a worshiper of God.

verses.kjv: And Crispus, the chief ruler of the synagogue, believed on the Lord with all his house; and many of the Corinthians hearing believed, and were baptized.
verses.bsb: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his whole household believed in the Lord. And many of the Corinthians who heard the message believed and were baptized.

verses.kjv: Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, Be not afraid, but speak, and hold not thy peace:
verses.bsb: One night the Lord spoke to Paul in a vision: “Do not be afraid; keep on speaking; do not be silent.

verses.kjv: For I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.
verses.bsb: For I am with you and no one will lay a hand on you, because I have many people in this city.”

verses.kjv: And he continued there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.
verses.bsb: So Paul stayed for a year and a half, teaching the word of God among the Corinthians.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Gallio was the deputy of Achaia, the Jews made insurrection with one accord against Paul, and brought him to the judgment seat,
verses.bsb: While Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews coordinated an attack on Paul and brought him before the judgment seat.

verses.kjv: Saying, This fellow persuadeth men to worship God contrary to the law.
verses.bsb: “This man is persuading the people to worship God in ways contrary to the law,” they said.

verses.kjv: And when Paul was now about to open his mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews, If it were a matter of wrong or wicked lewdness, O ye Jews, reason would that I should bear with you:
verses.bsb: But just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio told the Jews, “If this matter involved a wrongdoing or vicious crime, O Jews, it would be reasonable for me to hear your complaint.

verses.kjv: But if it be a question of words and names, and of your law, look ye to it; for I will be no judge of such matters.
verses.bsb: But since it is a dispute about words and names and your own law, settle it yourselves. I refuse to be a judge of such things.”

verses.kjv: And he drave them from the judgment seat.
verses.bsb: And he drove them away from the judgment seat.

verses.kjv: Then all the Greeks took Sosthenes, the chief ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. And Gallio cared for none of those things.
verses.bsb: At this, the crowd seized Sosthenes the synagogue leader and beat him in front of the judgment seat. But none of this was of concern to Gallio.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Paul after this tarried there yet a good while, and then took his leave of the brethren, and sailed thence into Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila; having shorn his head in Cenchrea: for he had a vow.
verses.bsb: Paul remained in Corinth for quite some time before saying goodbye to the brothers. He had his head shaved in Cenchrea to keep a vow he had made, and then he sailed for Syria, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila.

verses.kjv: And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.
verses.bsb: When they reached Ephesus, Paul parted ways with Priscilla and Aquila. He himself went into the synagogue there and reasoned with the Jews.

verses.kjv: When they desired him to tarry longer time with them, he consented not;
verses.bsb: When they asked him to stay for a while longer, he declined.

verses.kjv: But bade them farewell, saying, I must by all means keep this feast that cometh in Jerusalem: but I will return again unto you, if God will. And he sailed from Ephesus.
verses.bsb: But as he left, he said, “I will come back to you if God is willing.” And he set sail from Ephesus.

verses.kjv: And when he had landed at Cæsarea, and gone up, and saluted the church, he went down to Antioch.
verses.bsb: When Paul had landed at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the church at Jerusalem. Then he went down to Antioch.

verses.kjv: And after he had spent some time there, he departed, and went over all the country of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.
verses.bsb: After Paul had spent some time in Antioch, he traveled from place to place throughout the region of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.

verses.kjv: ¶ And a certain Jew named Apollos, born at Alexandria, an eloquent man, and mighty in the scriptures, came to Ephesus.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile a Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria, came to Ephesus. He was an eloquent man, well versed in the Scriptures.

verses.kjv: This man was instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in the spirit, he spake and taught diligently the things of the Lord, knowing only the baptism of John.
verses.bsb: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and was fervent in spirit. He spoke and taught accurately about Jesus, though he knew only the baptism of John.

verses.kjv: And he began to speak boldly in the synagogue: whom when Aquila and Priscilla had heard, they took him unto them, and expounded unto him the way of God more perfectly.
verses.bsb: And he began to speak boldly in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him in and explained to him the way of God more accurately.

verses.kjv: And when he was disposed to pass into Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receive him: who, when he was come, helped them much which had believed through grace:
verses.bsb: When Apollos resolved to cross over to Achaia, the brothers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples there to welcome him. On his arrival, he was a great help to those who by grace had believed.

verses.kjv: For he mightily convinced the Jews, and that publickly, shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was Christ.
verses.bsb: For he powerfully refuted the Jews in public debate, proving from the Scriptures that Jesus is the Christ.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that, while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul having passed through the upper coasts came to Ephesus: and finding certain disciples,
verses.bsb: While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul passed through the interior and came to Ephesus. There he found some disciples

verses.kjv: He said unto them, Have ye received the Holy Ghost since ye believed? And they said unto him, We have not so much as heard whether there be any Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: and asked them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you became believers?”  “No,” they answered, “we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they said, Unto John’s baptism.
verses.bsb: “Into what, then, were you baptized?” Paul asked.  “The baptism of John,” they replied.

verses.kjv: Then said Paul, John verily baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying unto the people, that they should believe on him which should come after him, that is, on Christ Jesus.
verses.bsb: Paul explained: “John’s baptism was a baptism of repentance. He told the people to believe in the One coming after him, that is, in Jesus.”

verses.kjv: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, they were baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.

verses.kjv: And when Paul had laid his hands upon them, the Holy Ghost came on them; and they spake with tongues, and prophesied.
verses.bsb: And when Paul laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied.

verses.kjv: And all the men were about twelve.
verses.bsb: There were about twelve men in all.

verses.kjv: And he went into the synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three months, disputing and persuading the things concerning the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Then Paul went into the synagogue and spoke boldly there for three months, arguing persuasively about the kingdom of God.

verses.kjv: But when divers were hardened, and believed not, but spake evil of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the school of one Tyrannus.
verses.bsb: But when some of them stubbornly refused to believe and publicly maligned the Way, Paul took his disciples and left the synagogue to conduct daily discussions in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.

verses.kjv: And this continued by the space of two years; so that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.
verses.bsb: This continued for two years, so that everyone who lived in the province of Asia, Jews and Greeks alike, heard the word of the Lord.

verses.kjv: And God wrought special miracles by the hands of Paul:
verses.bsb: God did extraordinary miracles through the hands of Paul,

verses.kjv: So that from his body were brought unto the sick handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out of them.
verses.bsb: so that even handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched him were taken to the sick, and the diseases and evil spirits left them.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then certain of the vagabond Jews, exorcists, took upon them to call over them which had evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preacheth.
verses.bsb: Now there were some itinerant Jewish exorcists who tried to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those with evil spirits. They would say, “I bind you by Jesus, whom Paul proclaims.”

verses.kjv: And there were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jew, and chief of the priests, which did so.
verses.bsb: Seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.

verses.kjv: And the evil spirit answered and said, Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are ye?
verses.bsb: Eventually, one of the evil spirits answered them, “Jesus I know, and I know about Paul, but who are you?”

verses.kjv: And the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, and overcame them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
verses.bsb: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all. The attack was so violent that they ran out of the house naked and wounded.

verses.kjv: And this was known to all the Jews and Greeks also dwelling at Ephesus; and fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.
verses.bsb: This became known to all the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus, and fear came over all of them. So the name of the Lord Jesus was held in high honor.

verses.kjv: And many that believed came, and confessed, and shewed their deeds.
verses.bsb: Many who had believed now came forward, confessing and disclosing their deeds.

verses.kjv: Many of them also which used curious arts brought their books together, and burned them before all men: and they counted the price of them, and found it fifty thousand pieces of silver.
verses.bsb: And a number of those who had practiced magic arts brought their books and burned them in front of everyone. When the value of the books was calculated, the total came to fifty thousand drachmas.

verses.kjv: So mightily grew the word of God and prevailed.
verses.bsb: So the word of the Lord powerfully continued to spread and prevail.

verses.kjv: ¶ After these things were ended, Paul purposed in the spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, After I have been there, I must also see Rome.
verses.bsb: After these things had happened, Paul resolved in the Spirit to go to Jerusalem after he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia. “After I have been there,” he said, “I must see Rome as well.”

verses.kjv: So he sent into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timotheus and Erastus; but he himself stayed in Asia for a season.
verses.bsb: He sent two of his helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he stayed for a time in the province of Asia.

verses.kjv: And the same time there arose no small stir about that way.
verses.bsb: About that time there arose a great disturbance about the Way.

verses.kjv: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith, which made silver shrines for Diana, brought no small gain unto the craftsmen;
verses.bsb: It began with a silversmith named Demetrius who made silver shrines of Artemis, bringing much business to the craftsmen.

verses.kjv: Whom he called together with the workmen of like occupation, and said, Sirs, ye know that by this craft we have our wealth.
verses.bsb: Demetrius assembled the craftsmen, along with the workmen in related trades. “Men,” he said, “you know that this business is our source of prosperity.

verses.kjv: Moreover ye see and hear, that not alone at Ephesus, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul hath persuaded and turned away much people, saying that they be no gods, which are made with hands:
verses.bsb: And you can see and hear that not only in Ephesus, but in nearly the whole province of Asia, Paul has persuaded a great number of people to turn away. He says that man-made gods are no gods at all.

verses.kjv: So that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought; but also that the temple of the great goddess Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worshippeth.
verses.bsb: There is danger not only that our business will fall into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be discredited and her majesty deposed—she who is worshiped by all the province of Asia and the whole world.”

verses.kjv: And when they heard these sayings, they were full of wrath, and cried out, saying, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
verses.bsb: When the men heard this, they were enraged and began shouting, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

verses.kjv: And the whole city was filled with confusion: and having caught Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul’s companions in travel, they rushed with one accord into the theatre.
verses.bsb: Soon the whole city was in disarray. They rushed together into the theatre, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Paul’s traveling companions from Macedonia.

verses.kjv: And when Paul would have entered in unto the people, the disciples suffered him not.
verses.bsb: Paul wanted to go before the assembly, but the disciples would not allow him.

verses.kjv: And certain of the chief of Asia, which were his friends, sent unto him, desiring him that he would not adventure himself into the theatre.
verses.bsb: Even some of Paul’s friends who were officials of the province of Asia sent word to him, begging him not to venture into the theatre.

verses.kjv: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another: for the assembly was confused; and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile the assembly was in turmoil. Some were shouting one thing and some another, and most of them did not even know why they were there.

verses.kjv: And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. And Alexander beckoned with the hand, and would have made his defence unto the people.
verses.bsb: The Jews in the crowd pushed Alexander forward to explain himself, and he motioned for silence so he could make his defense to the people.

verses.kjv: But when they knew that he was a Jew, all with one voice about the space of two hours cried out, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
verses.bsb: But when they realized that he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison for about two hours: “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

verses.kjv: And when the townclerk had appeased the people, he said, Ye men of Ephesus, what man is there that knoweth not how that the city of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddess Diana, and of the image which fell down from Jupiter?
verses.bsb: Finally the city clerk quieted the crowd and declared, “Men of Ephesus, doesn’t everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?

verses.kjv: Seeing then that these things cannot be spoken against, ye ought to be quiet, and to do nothing rashly.
verses.bsb: Since these things are undeniable, you ought to be calm and not do anything rash.

verses.kjv: For ye have brought hither these men, which are neither robbers of churches, nor yet blasphemers of your goddess.
verses.bsb: For you have brought these men here, though they have neither robbed our temple nor blasphemed our goddess.

verses.kjv: Wherefore if Demetrius, and the craftsmen which are with him, have a matter against any man, the law is open, and there are deputies: let them implead one another.
verses.bsb: So if Demetrius and his fellow craftsmen have a complaint against anyone, the courts are open and proconsuls are available. Let them bring charges against one another there.

verses.kjv: But if ye inquire any thing concerning other matters, it shall be determined in a lawful assembly.
verses.bsb: But if you are seeking anything beyond this, it must be settled in a legal assembly.

verses.kjv: For we are in danger to be called in question for this day’s uproar, there being no cause whereby we may give an account of this concourse.
verses.bsb: For we are in jeopardy of being charged with rioting for today’s events, and we have no justification to account for this commotion.”

verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.
verses.bsb: After he had said this, he dismissed the assembly.

verses.kjv: AND after the uproar was ceased, Paul called unto him the disciples, and embraced them, and departed for to go into Macedonia.
verses.bsb: When the uproar had ended, Paul sent for the disciples. And after encouraging them, he said goodbye to them and left for Macedonia.

verses.kjv: And when he had gone over those parts, and had given them much exhortation, he came into Greece,
verses.bsb: After traveling through that area and speaking many words of encouragement, he arrived in Greece,

verses.kjv: And there abode three months. And when the Jews laid wait for him, as he was about to sail into Syria, he purposed to return through Macedonia.
verses.bsb: where he stayed three months. And when the Jews formed a plot against him as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided to go back through Macedonia.

verses.kjv: And there accompanied him into Asia Sopater of Berea; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus; and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
verses.bsb: Paul was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.

verses.kjv: These going before tarried for us at Troas.
verses.bsb: These men went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.

verses.kjv: And we sailed away from Philippi after the days of unleavened bread, and came unto them to Troas in five days; where we abode seven days.
verses.bsb: And after the Feast of Unleavened Bread, we sailed from Philippi, and five days later we rejoined them in Troas, where we stayed seven days.

verses.kjv: And upon the first day of the week, when the disciples came together to break bread, Paul preached unto them, ready to depart on the morrow; and continued his speech until midnight.
verses.bsb: On the first day of the week we came together to break bread. Since Paul was ready to leave the next day, he talked to them and kept on speaking until midnight.

verses.kjv: And there were many lights in the upper chamber, where they were gathered together.
verses.bsb: Now there were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered.

verses.kjv: And there sat in a window a certain young man named Eutychus, being fallen into a deep sleep: and as Paul was long preaching, he sunk down with sleep, and fell down from the third loft, and was taken up dead.
verses.bsb: And a certain young man named Eutychus, seated in the window, was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third story and was picked up dead.

verses.kjv: And Paul went down, and fell on him, and embracing him said, Trouble not yourselves; for his life is in him.
verses.bsb: But Paul went down, threw himself on the young man, and embraced him. “Do not be alarmed!” he said. “He is still alive!”

verses.kjv: When he therefore was come up again, and had broken bread, and eaten, and talked a long while, even till break of day, so he departed.
verses.bsb: Then Paul went back upstairs, broke bread, and ate. And after speaking until daybreak, he departed.

verses.kjv: And they brought the young man alive, and were not a little comforted.
verses.bsb: And the people were greatly relieved to take the boy home alive.

verses.kjv: ¶ And we went before to ship, and sailed unto Assos, there intending to take in Paul: for so had he appointed, minding himself to go afoot.
verses.bsb: We went on ahead to the ship and sailed to Assos, where we were to take Paul aboard. He had arranged this because he was going there on foot.

verses.kjv: And when he met with us at Assos, we took him in, and came to Mitylene.
verses.bsb: And when he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.

verses.kjv: And we sailed thence, and came the next day over against Chios; and the next day we arrived at Samos, and tarried at Trogyllium; and the next day we came to Miletus.
verses.bsb: Sailing on from there, we arrived the next day opposite Chios. The day after that we arrived at Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.

verses.kjv: For Paul had determined to sail by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Jerusalem the day of Pentecost.
verses.bsb: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus to avoid spending time in the province of Asia, because he was in a hurry to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by the day of Pentecost.

verses.kjv: ¶ And from Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called the elders of the church.
verses.bsb: From Miletus, Paul sent to Ephesus for the elders of the church.

verses.kjv: And when they were come to him, he said unto them, Ye know, from the first day that I came into Asia, after what manner I have been with you at all seasons,
verses.bsb: When they came to him, he said, “You know how I lived the whole time I was with you, from the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.

verses.kjv: Serving the Lord with all humility of mind, and with many tears, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Jews:
verses.bsb: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears, especially in the trials that came upon me through the plots of the Jews.

verses.kjv: And how I kept back nothing that was profitable unto you, but have shewed you, and have taught you publickly, and from house to house,
verses.bsb: I did not shrink back from declaring anything that was helpful to you as I taught you publicly and from house to house,

verses.kjv: Testifying both to the Jews, and also to the Greeks, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: testifying to Jews and Greeks alike about repentance to God and faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: And now, behold, I go bound in the spirit unto Jerusalem, not knowing the things that shall befall me there:
verses.bsb: And now, compelled by the Spirit, I am going to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there.

verses.kjv: Save that the Holy Ghost witnesseth in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions abide me.
verses.bsb: I only know that in town after town the Holy Spirit warns me that chains and afflictions await me.

verses.kjv: But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God.
verses.bsb: But I consider my life of no value to me, if only I may finish my course and complete the ministry I have received from the Lord Jesus—the ministry of testifying to the good news of God’s grace.

verses.kjv: And now, behold, I know that ye all, among whom I have gone preaching the kingdom of God, shall see my face no more.
verses.bsb: Now I know that none of you among whom I have preached the kingdom will see my face again.

verses.kjv: Wherefore I take you to record this day, that I am pure from the blood of all men.
verses.bsb: Therefore I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all men.

verses.kjv: For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.
verses.bsb: For I did not shrink back from declaring to you the whole will of God.

verses.kjv: ¶ Take heed therefore unto yourselves, and to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers, to feed the church of God, which he hath purchased with his own blood.
verses.bsb: Keep watch over yourselves and the entire flock of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers. Be shepherds of the church of God, which He purchased with His own blood.

verses.kjv: For I know this, that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.
verses.bsb: I know that after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and will not spare the flock.

verses.kjv: Also of your own selves shall men arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away disciples after them.
verses.bsb: Even from your own number, men will rise up and distort the truth to draw away disciples after them.

verses.kjv: Therefore watch, and remember, that by the space of three years I ceased not to warn every one night and day with tears.
verses.bsb: Therefore be alert and remember that for three years I never stopped warning each of you night and day with tears.

verses.kjv: And now, brethren, I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up, and to give you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified.
verses.bsb: And now I commit you to God and to the word of His grace, which can build you up and give you an inheritance among all who are sanctified.

verses.kjv: I have coveted no man’s silver, or gold, or apparel.
verses.bsb: I have not coveted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing.

verses.kjv: Yea, ye yourselves know, that these hands have ministered unto my necessities, and to them that were with me.
verses.bsb: You yourselves know that these hands of mine have ministered to my own needs and those of my companions.

verses.kjv: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.
verses.bsb: In everything, I showed you that by this kind of hard work we must help the weak, remembering the words of the Lord Jesus Himself: ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had thus spoken, he kneeled down, and prayed with them all.
verses.bsb: When Paul had said this, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.

verses.kjv: And they all wept sore, and fell on Paul’s neck, and kissed him,
verses.bsb: They all wept openly as they embraced Paul and kissed him.

verses.kjv: Sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
verses.bsb: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see his face again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that after we were gotten from them, and had launched, we came with a straight course unto Coos, and the day following unto Rhodes, and from thence unto Patara:
verses.bsb: After we had torn ourselves away from them, we sailed directly to Cos, and the next day on to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.

verses.kjv: And finding a ship sailing over unto Phenicia, we went aboard, and set forth.
verses.bsb: Finding a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, we boarded it and set sail.

verses.kjv: Now when we had discovered Cyprus, we left it on the left hand, and sailed into Syria, and landed at Tyre: for there the ship was to unlade her burden.
verses.bsb: After sighting Cyprus and passing south of it, we sailed on to Syria and landed at Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.

verses.kjv: And finding disciples, we tarried there seven days: who said to Paul through the Spirit, that he should not go up to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: We sought out the disciples in Tyre and stayed with them seven days. Through the Spirit they kept telling Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And when we had accomplished those days, we departed and went our way; and they all brought us on our way, with wives and children, till we were out of the city: and we kneeled down on the shore, and prayed.
verses.bsb: But when our time there had ended, we set out on our journey. All the disciples, with their wives and children, accompanied us out of the city and knelt down on the beach to pray with us.

verses.kjv: And when we had taken our leave one of another, we took ship; and they returned home again.
verses.bsb: And after we had said our farewells, we went aboard the ship, and they returned home.

verses.kjv: And when we had finished our course from Tyre, we came to Ptolemais, and saluted the brethren, and abode with them one day.
verses.bsb: When we had finished our voyage from Tyre, we landed at Ptolemais, where we greeted the brothers and stayed with them for a day.

verses.kjv: And the next day we that were of Paul’s company departed, and came unto Cæsarea: and we entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, which was one of the seven; and abode with him.
verses.bsb: Leaving the next day, we went on to Caesarea and stayed at the home of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the Seven.

verses.kjv: And the same man had four daughters, virgins, which did prophesy.
verses.bsb: He had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.

verses.kjv: And as we tarried there many days, there came down from Judæa a certain prophet, named Agabus.
verses.bsb: After we had been there several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.

verses.kjv: And when he was come unto us, he took Paul’s girdle, and bound his own hands and feet, and said, Thus saith the Holy Ghost, So shall the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man that owneth this girdle, and shall deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: Coming over to us, he took Paul’s belt, bound his own feet and hands, and said, “The Holy Spirit says: ‘In this way the Jews of Jerusalem will bind the owner of this belt and hand him over to the Gentiles.’”

verses.kjv: And when we heard these things, both we, and they of that place, besought him not to go up to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: When we heard this, we and the people there pleaded with Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Then Paul answered, What mean ye to weep and to break mine heart? for I am ready not to be bound only, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then Paul answered, “Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”

verses.kjv: And when he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, The will of the Lord be done.
verses.bsb: When he would not be dissuaded, we fell silent and said, “The Lord’s will be done.”

verses.kjv: And after those days we took up our carriages, and went up to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: After these days, we packed up and went on to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: There went with us also certain of the disciples of Cæsarea, and brought with them one Mnason of Cyprus, an old disciple, with whom we should lodge.
verses.bsb: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us, and they took us to stay at the home of Mnason the Cypriot, an early disciple.

verses.kjv: And when we were come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.
verses.bsb: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers welcomed us joyfully.

verses.kjv: And the day following Paul went in with us unto James; and all the elders were present.
verses.bsb: The next day Paul went in with us to see James, and all the elders were present.

verses.kjv: And when he had saluted them, he declared particularly what things God had wrought among the Gentiles by his ministry.
verses.bsb: Paul greeted them and recounted one by one the things that God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.

verses.kjv: And when they heard it, they glorified the Lord, and said unto him, Thou seest, brother, how many thousands of Jews there are which believe; and they are all zealous of the law:
verses.bsb: When they heard this, they glorified God. Then they said to Paul, “You see, brother, how many thousands of Jews have believed, and all of them are zealous for the law.

verses.kjv: And they are informed of thee, that thou teachest all the Jews which are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, saying that they ought not to circumcise their children, neither to walk after the customs.
verses.bsb: But they are under the impression that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children or observe our customs.

verses.kjv: What is it therefore? the multitude must needs come together: for they will hear that thou art come.
verses.bsb: What then should we do? They will certainly hear that you have come.

verses.kjv: Do therefore this that we say to thee: We have four men which have a vow on them;
verses.bsb: Therefore do what we advise you. There are four men with us who have taken a vow.

verses.kjv: Them take, and purify thyself with them, and be at charges with them, that they may shave their heads: and all may know that those things, whereof they were informed concerning thee, are nothing; but that thou thyself also walkest orderly, and keepest the law.
verses.bsb: Take these men, purify yourself along with them, and pay their expenses so they can have their heads shaved. Then everyone will know that there is no truth to these rumors about you, but that you also live in obedience to the law.

verses.kjv: As touching the Gentiles which believe, we have written and concluded that they observe no such thing, save only that they keep themselves from things offered to idols, and from blood, and from strangled, and from fornication.
verses.bsb: As for the Gentile believers, we have written to them our decision that they must abstain from food sacrificed to idols, from blood, from the meat of strangled animals, and from sexual immorality.”

verses.kjv: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purifying himself with them entered into the temple, to signify the accomplishment of the days of purification, until that an offering should be offered for every one of them.
verses.bsb: So the next day Paul took the men and purified himself along with them. Then he entered the temple to give notice of the date when their purification would be complete and the offering would be made for each of them.

verses.kjv: And when the seven days were almost ended, the Jews which were of Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the people, and laid hands on him,
verses.bsb: When the seven days were almost over, some Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul at the temple. They stirred up the whole crowd and seized him,

verses.kjv: Crying out, Men of Israel, help: This is the man, that teacheth all men every where against the people, and the law, and this place: and further brought Greeks also into the temple, and hath polluted this holy place.
verses.bsb: crying out, “Men of Israel, help us! This is the man who teaches everywhere against our people and against our law and against this place. Furthermore, he has brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place.”

verses.kjv: (For they had seen before with him in the city Trophimus an Ephesian, whom they supposed that Paul had brought into the temple.)
verses.bsb: For they had previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they assumed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

verses.kjv: And all the city was moved, and the people ran together: and they took Paul, and drew him out of the temple: and forthwith the doors were shut.
verses.bsb: The whole city was stirred up, and the people rushed together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple, and at once the gates were shut.

verses.kjv: And as they went about to kill him, tidings came unto the chief captain of the band, that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.
verses.bsb: While they were trying to kill him, the commander of the Roman regiment received a report that all Jerusalem was in turmoil.

verses.kjv: Who immediately took soldiers and centurions, and ran down unto them: and when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, they left beating of Paul.
verses.bsb: Immediately he took some soldiers and centurions and ran down to the crowd. When the people saw the commander and the soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.

verses.kjv: Then the chief captain came near, and took him, and commanded him to be bound with two chains; and demanded who he was, and what he had done.
verses.bsb: The commander came up and arrested Paul, ordering that he be bound with two chains. Then he asked who he was and what he had done.

verses.kjv: And some cried one thing, some another, among the multitude: and when he could not know the certainty for the tumult, he commanded him to be carried into the castle.
verses.bsb: Some in the crowd were shouting one thing, and some another. And since the commander could not get at the truth because of the uproar, he ordered that Paul be brought into the barracks.

verses.kjv: And when he came upon the stairs, so it was, that he was borne of the soldiers for the violence of the people.
verses.bsb: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the mob.

verses.kjv: For the multitude of the people followed after, crying, Away with him.
verses.bsb: For the crowd that followed him kept shouting, “Away with him!”

verses.kjv: And as Paul was to be led into the castle, he said unto the chief captain, May I speak unto thee? Who said, Canst thou speak Greek?
verses.bsb: As they were about to take Paul into the barracks, he asked the commander, “May I say something to you?”  “Do you speak Greek?” he replied.

verses.kjv: Art not thou that Egyptian, which before these days madest an uproar, and leddest out into the wilderness four thousand men that were murderers?
verses.bsb: “Aren’t you the Egyptian who incited a rebellion some time ago and led four thousand members of the ‘Assassins’ into the wilderness?”

verses.kjv: But Paul said, I am a man which am a Jew of Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, a citizen of no mean city: and, I beseech thee, suffer me to speak unto the people.
verses.bsb: But Paul answered, “I am a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no ordinary city. Now I beg you to allow me to speak to the people.”

verses.kjv: And when he had given him licence, Paul stood on the stairs, and beckoned with the hand unto the people. And when there was made a great silence, he spake unto them in the Hebrew tongue, saying,
verses.bsb: Having received permission, Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the crowd. A great hush came over the crowd, and he addressed them in Hebrew:

verses.kjv: MEN, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence which I make now unto you.
verses.bsb: “Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense before you.”

verses.kjv: (And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)
verses.bsb: When they heard him speak to them in Hebrew, they became even more silent.  Then Paul declared,

verses.kjv: I am verily a man which am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.
verses.bsb: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but raised in this city. I was educated at the feet of Gamaliel in strict conformity to the law of our fathers. I am just as zealous for God as any of you here today.

verses.kjv: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.
verses.bsb: I persecuted this Way even to the death, detaining both men and women and throwing them into prison,

verses.kjv: As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.
verses.bsb: as the high priest and the whole Council can testify about me. I even obtained letters from them to their brothers in Damascus, and I was on my way to apprehend these people and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.
verses.bsb: About noon as I was approaching Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven flashed around me.

verses.kjv: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
verses.bsb: I fell to the ground and heard a voice say to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me?’

verses.kjv: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.
verses.bsb: ‘Who are You, Lord?’ I asked.  ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting,’ He replied.

verses.kjv: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
verses.bsb: My companions saw the light, but they could not understand the voice of the One speaking to me.

verses.kjv: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
verses.bsb: Then I asked, ‘What should I do, Lord?’  ‘Get up and go into Damascus,’ He told me. ‘There you will be told all that you have been appointed to do.’

verses.kjv: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
verses.bsb: Because the brilliance of the light had blinded me, my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.

verses.kjv: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
verses.bsb: There a man named Ananias, a devout observer of the law who was highly regarded by all the Jews living there,

verses.kjv: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.
verses.bsb: came and stood beside me. ‘Brother Saul,’ he said, ‘receive your sight.’ And at that moment I could see him.

verses.kjv: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
verses.bsb: Then he said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know His will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear His voice.

verses.kjv: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
verses.bsb: You will be His witness to everyone of what you have seen and heard.

verses.kjv: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash your sins away, calling on His name.’

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
verses.bsb: Later, when I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying at the temple, I fell into a trance

verses.kjv: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
verses.bsb: and saw the Lord saying to me, ‘Hurry! Leave Jerusalem quickly, because the people here will not accept your testimony about Me.’

verses.kjv: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
verses.bsb: ‘Lord,’ I answered, ‘they know very well that in one synagogue after another I imprisoned and beat those who believed in You.

verses.kjv: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
verses.bsb: And when the blood of Your witness Stephen was shed, I stood there giving my approval and watching over the garments of those who killed him.’

verses.kjv: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: Then He said to me, ‘Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”

verses.kjv: And they gave him audience unto this word, and then lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
verses.bsb: The crowd listened to Paul until he made this statement. Then they lifted up their voices and shouted, “Rid the earth of him! He is not fit to live!”

verses.kjv: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
verses.bsb: As they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust into the air,

verses.kjv: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
verses.bsb: the commander ordered that Paul be brought into the barracks. He directed that Paul be flogged and interrogated to determine the reason for this outcry against him.

verses.kjv: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
verses.bsb: But as they stretched him out to strap him down, Paul said to the centurion standing there, “Is it lawful for you to flog a Roman citizen without a trial?”

verses.kjv: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the chief captain, saying, Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, the centurion went and reported it to the commander. “What are you going to do?” he said. “This man is a Roman citizen.”

verses.kjv: Then the chief captain came, and said unto him, Tell me, art thou a Roman? He said, Yea.
verses.bsb: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?”  “Yes,” he answered.

verses.kjv: And the chief captain answered, With a great sum obtained I this freedom. And Paul said, But I was free born.
verses.bsb: “I paid a high price for my citizenship,” said the commander.  “But I was born a citizen,” Paul replied.

verses.kjv: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.
verses.bsb: Then those who were about to interrogate Paul stepped back, and the commander himself was alarmed when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains.

verses.kjv: On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
verses.bsb: The next day the commander, wanting to learn the real reason Paul was accused by the Jews, released him and ordered the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin to assemble. Then he brought Paul down and had him stand before them.

verses.kjv: AND Paul, earnestly beholding the council, said, Men and brethren, I have lived in all good conscience before God until this day.
verses.bsb: Paul looked directly at the Sanhedrin and said, “Brothers, I have conducted myself before God in all good conscience to this day.”

verses.kjv: And the high priest Ananias commanded them that stood by him to smite him on the mouth.
verses.bsb: At this, the high priest Ananias ordered those standing near Paul to strike him on the mouth.

verses.kjv: Then said Paul unto him, God shall smite thee, thou whited wall: for sittest thou to judge me after the law, and commandest me to be smitten contrary to the law?
verses.bsb: Then Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit here to judge me according to the law, yet you yourself violate the law by commanding that I be struck.”

verses.kjv: And they that stood by said, Revilest thou God’s high priest?
verses.bsb: But those standing nearby said, “How dare you insult the high priest of God!”

verses.kjv: Then said Paul, I wist not, brethren, that he was the high priest: for it is written, Thou shalt not speak evil of the ruler of thy people.
verses.bsb: “Brothers,” Paul replied, “I was not aware that he was the high priest, for it is written: ‘Do not speak evil about the ruler of your people.’”

verses.kjv: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees, and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, Men and brethren, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee: of the hope and resurrection of the dead I am called in question.
verses.bsb: Then Paul, knowing that some of them were Sadducees and others Pharisees, called out in the Sanhedrin, “Brothers, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee. It is because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial.”

verses.kjv: And when he had so said, there arose a dissension between the Pharisees and the Sadducees: and the multitude was divided.
verses.bsb: As soon as he had said this, a dispute broke out between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.

verses.kjv: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, neither angel, nor spirit: but the Pharisees confess both.
verses.bsb: For the Sadducees say that there is neither a resurrection, nor angels, nor spirits, but the Pharisees acknowledge them all.

verses.kjv: And there arose a great cry: and the scribes that were of the Pharisees’ part arose, and strove, saying, We find no evil in this man: but if a spirit or an angel hath spoken to him, let us not fight against God.
verses.bsb: A great clamor arose, and some scribes from the party of the Pharisees got up and contended sharply, “We find nothing wrong with this man. What if a spirit or an angel has spoken to him?”

verses.kjv: And when there arose a great dissension, the chief captain, fearing lest Paul should have been pulled in pieces of them, commanded the soldiers to go down, and to take him by force from among them, and to bring him into the castle.
verses.bsb: The dispute grew so violent that the commander was afraid they would tear Paul to pieces. He ordered the soldiers to go down and remove him by force and bring him into the barracks.

verses.kjv: And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.
verses.bsb: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, “Take courage! As you have testified about Me in Jerusalem, so also you must testify in Rome.”

verses.kjv: And when it was day, certain of the Jews banded together, and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they had killed Paul.
verses.bsb: When daylight came, the Jews formed a conspiracy and bound themselves with an oath not to eat or drink until they had killed Paul.

verses.kjv: And they were more than forty which had made this conspiracy.
verses.bsb: More than forty of them were involved in this plot.

verses.kjv: And they came to the chief priests and elders, and said, We have bound ourselves under a great curse, that we will eat nothing until we have slain Paul.
verses.bsb: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, “We have bound ourselves with a solemn oath not to eat anything until we have killed Paul.

verses.kjv: Now therefore ye with the council signify to the chief captain that he bring him down unto you to morrow, as though ye would inquire something more perfectly concerning him: and we, or ever he come near, are ready to kill him.
verses.bsb: Now then, you and the Sanhedrin petition the commander to bring him down to you on the pretext of examining his case more carefully. We are ready to kill him on the way.”

verses.kjv: And when Paul’s sister’s son heard of their lying in wait, he went and entered into the castle, and told Paul.
verses.bsb: But when the son of Paul’s sister heard about the plot, he went into the barracks and told Paul.

verses.kjv: Then Paul called one of the centurions unto him, and said, Bring this young man unto the chief captain: for he hath a certain thing to tell him.
verses.bsb: Then Paul called one of the centurions and said, “Take this young man to the commander; he has something to tell him.”

verses.kjv: So he took him, and brought him to the chief captain, and said, Paul the prisoner called me unto him, and prayed me to bring this young man unto thee, who hath something to say unto thee.
verses.bsb: So the centurion took him to the commander and said, “Paul the prisoner sent and asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.”

verses.kjv: Then the chief captain took him by the hand, and went with him aside privately, and asked him, What is that thou hast to tell me?
verses.bsb: The commander took the young man by the hand, drew him aside, and asked, “What do you need to tell me?”

verses.kjv: And he said, The Jews have agreed to desire thee that thou wouldest bring down Paul to morrow into the council, as though they would inquire somewhat of him more perfectly.
verses.bsb: He answered, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul to the Sanhedrin tomorrow on the pretext of acquiring more information about him.

verses.kjv: But do not thou yield unto them: for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men, which have bound themselves with an oath, that they will neither eat nor drink till they have killed him: and now are they ready, looking for a promise from thee.
verses.bsb: Do not let them persuade you, because more than forty men are waiting to ambush him. They have bound themselves with an oath not to eat or drink until they have killed him; they are ready now, awaiting your consent.”

verses.kjv: So the chief captain then let the young man depart, and charged him, See thou tell no man that thou hast shewed these things to me.
verses.bsb: So the commander dismissed the young man and instructed him, “Do not tell anyone that you have reported this to me.”

verses.kjv: And he called unto him two centurions, saying, Make ready two hundred soldiers to go to Cæsarea, and horsemen threescore and ten, and spearmen two hundred, at the third hour of the night;
verses.bsb: Then he called two of his centurions and said, “Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to go to Caesarea in the third hour of the night.

verses.kjv: And provide them beasts, that they may set Paul on, and bring him safe unto Felix the governor.
verses.bsb: Provide mounts for Paul to take him safely to Governor Felix.”

verses.kjv: And he wrote a letter after this manner:
verses.bsb: And he wrote the following letter:

verses.kjv: Claudius Lysias unto the most excellent governor Felix sendeth greeting.
verses.bsb: Claudius Lysias,  To His Excellency, Governor Felix:  Greetings.

verses.kjv: This man was taken of the Jews, and should have been killed of them: then came I with an army, and rescued him, having understood that he was a Roman.
verses.bsb: This man was seized by the Jews, and they were about to kill him when I came with my troops to rescue him. For I had learned that he is a Roman citizen,

verses.kjv: And when I would have known the cause wherefore they accused him, I brought him forth into their council:
verses.bsb: and since I wanted to understand their charges against him, I brought him down to their Sanhedrin.

verses.kjv: Whom I perceived to be accused of questions of their law, but to have nothing laid to his charge worthy of death or of bonds.
verses.bsb: I found that the accusation involved questions about their own law, but there was no charge worthy of death or imprisonment.

verses.kjv: And when it was told me how that the Jews laid wait for the man, I sent straightway to thee, and gave commandment to his accusers also to say before thee what they had against him. Farewell.
verses.bsb: When I was informed that there was a plot against the man, I sent him to you at once. I also instructed his accusers to present their case against him before you.

verses.kjv: Then the soldiers, as it was commanded them, took Paul, and brought him by night to Antipatris.
verses.bsb: So the soldiers followed their orders and brought Paul by night to Antipatris.

verses.kjv: On the morrow they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the castle:
verses.bsb: The next day they returned to the barracks and let the horsemen go on with him.

verses.kjv: Who, when they came to Cæsarea, and delivered the epistle to the governor, presented Paul also before him.
verses.bsb: When the horsemen arrived in Caesarea, they delivered the letter to the governor and presented Paul to him.

verses.kjv: And when the governor had read the letter, he asked of what province he was. And when he understood that he was of Cilicia;
verses.bsb: The governor read the letter and asked what province Paul was from. Learning that he was from Cilicia,

verses.kjv: I will hear thee, said he, when thine accusers are also come. And he commanded him to be kept in Herod’s judgment hall.
verses.bsb: he said, “I will hear your case when your accusers arrive.” Then he ordered that Paul be kept under guard in Herod’s Praetorium.

verses.kjv: AND after five days Ananias the high priest descended with the elders, and with a certain orator named Tertullus, who informed the governor against Paul.
verses.bsb: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus, who presented to the governor their case against Paul.

verses.kjv: And when he was called forth, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, Seeing that by thee we enjoy great quietness, and that very worthy deeds are done unto this nation by thy providence,
verses.bsb: When Paul had been called in, Tertullus opened the prosecution: “Because of you, we have enjoyed a lasting peace, and your foresight has brought improvements to this nation.

verses.kjv: We accept it always, and in all places, most noble Felix, with all thankfulness.
verses.bsb: In every way and everywhere, most excellent Felix, we acknowledge this with all gratitude.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding, that I be not further tedious unto thee, I pray thee that thou wouldest hear us of thy clemency a few words.
verses.bsb: But in order not to burden you any further, I beg your indulgence to hear us briefly.

verses.kjv: For we have found this man a pestilent fellow, and a mover of sedition among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes:
verses.bsb: We have found this man to be a pestilence, stirring up dissension among the Jews all over the world. He is a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes,

verses.kjv: Who also hath gone about to profane the temple: whom we took, and would have judged according to our law.
verses.bsb: and he even tried to desecrate the temple; so we seized him.

verses.kjv: Commanding his accusers to come unto thee: by examining of whom thyself mayest take knowledge of all these things, whereof we accuse him.
verses.bsb: By examining him yourself, you will be able to learn the truth about all our charges against him.”

verses.kjv: And the Jews also assented, saying that these things were so.
verses.bsb: The Jews concurred, asserting that these charges were true.

verses.kjv: Then Paul, after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak, answered, Forasmuch as I know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself:
verses.bsb: When the governor motioned for Paul to speak, he began his response: “Knowing that you have been a judge over this nation for many years, I gladly make my defense.

verses.kjv: Because that thou mayest understand, that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to worship.
verses.bsb: You can verify for yourself that no more than twelve days ago I went up to Jerusalem to worship.

verses.kjv: And they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man, neither raising up the people, neither in the synagogues, nor in the city:
verses.bsb: Yet my accusers did not find me debating with anyone in the temple or riling up a crowd in the synagogues or in the city.

verses.kjv: Neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me.
verses.bsb: Nor can they prove to you any of their charges against me.

verses.kjv: But this I confess unto thee, that after the way which they call heresy, so worship I the God of my fathers, believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets:
verses.bsb: I do confess to you, however, that I worship the God of our fathers according to the Way, which they call a sect. I believe everything that is laid down by the Law and written in the Prophets,

verses.kjv: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.
verses.bsb: and I have the same hope in God that they themselves cherish, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the wicked.

verses.kjv: And herein do I exercise myself, to have always a conscience void of offence toward God, and toward men.
verses.bsb: In this hope, I strive always to maintain a clear conscience before God and man.

verses.kjv: Now after many years I came to bring alms to my nation, and offerings.
verses.bsb: After several years, then, I returned to Jerusalem to bring alms to my people and to present offerings.

verses.kjv: Whereupon certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, neither with multitude, nor with tumult.
verses.bsb: At the time they found me in the temple, I was ceremonially clean and was not inciting a crowd or an uproar. But there are some Jews from the province of Asia

verses.kjv: Who ought to have been here before thee, and object, if they had ought against me.
verses.bsb: who ought to appear before you and bring charges, if they have anything against me.

verses.kjv: Or else let these same here say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,
verses.bsb: Otherwise, let these men state for themselves any crime they found in me when I stood before the Sanhedrin,

verses.kjv: Except it be for this one voice, that I cried standing among them, Touching the resurrection of the dead I am called in question by you this day.
verses.bsb: unless it was this one thing I called out as I stood in their presence: ‘It is concerning the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial before you today.’”

verses.kjv: And when Felix heard these things, having more perfect knowledge of that way, he deferred them, and said, When Lysias the chief captain shall come down, I will know the uttermost of your matter.
verses.bsb: Then Felix, who was well informed about the Way, adjourned the hearing and said, “When Lysias the commander comes, I will decide your case.”

verses.kjv: And he commanded a centurion to keep Paul, and to let him have liberty, and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him.
verses.bsb: He ordered the centurion to keep Paul under guard, but to allow him some freedom and permit his friends to minister to his needs.

verses.kjv: And after certain days, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, which was a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.
verses.bsb: After several days, Felix returned with his wife Drusilla, who was a Jewess. He sent for Paul and listened to him speak about faith in Christ Jesus.

verses.kjv: And as he reasoned of righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come, Felix trembled, and answered, Go thy way for this time; when I have a convenient season, I will call for thee.
verses.bsb: As Paul expounded on righteousness, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became frightened and said, “You may go for now. When I find the time, I will call for you.”

verses.kjv: He hoped also that money should have been given him of Paul, that he might loose him: wherefore he sent for him the oftener, and communed with him.
verses.bsb: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him a bribe. So he sent for Paul frequently and talked with him.

verses.kjv: But after two years Porcius Festus came into Felix’ room: and Felix, willing to shew the Jews a pleasure, left Paul bound.
verses.bsb: After two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. And wishing to do the Jews a favor, Felix left Paul in prison.

verses.kjv: NOW when Festus was come into the province, after three days he ascended from Cæsarea to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Three days after his arrival in the province, Festus went up from Caesarea to Jerusalem,

verses.kjv: Then the high priest and the chief of the Jews informed him against Paul, and besought him,
verses.bsb: where the chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. They urged Festus

verses.kjv: And desired favour against him, that he would send for him to Jerusalem, laying wait in the way to kill him.
verses.bsb: to grant them a concession against Paul by summoning him to Jerusalem, because they were preparing an ambush to kill him along the way.

verses.kjv: But Festus answered, that Paul should be kept at Cæsarea, and that he himself would depart shortly thither.
verses.bsb: But Festus replied, “Paul is being held in Caesarea, and I myself am going there soon.

verses.kjv: Let them therefore, said he, which among you are able, go down with me, and accuse this man, if there be any wickedness in him.
verses.bsb: So if this man has done anything wrong, let some of your leaders come down with me and accuse him there.”

verses.kjv: And when he had tarried among them more than ten days, he went down unto Cæsarea; and the next day sitting on the judgment seat commanded Paul to be brought.
verses.bsb: After spending no more than eight or ten days with them, Festus went down to Caesarea. The next day he sat on the judgment seat and ordered that Paul be brought in.
